prose:
Method–provenance block
Property: Fukui reactivity indices (f⁺, f⁻, f⁰, dual descriptor) and identification of the most electrophilic/nucleophilic atoms in acrolein (gas phase).
Level of theory: DFT, functional B3LYP with basis def2-TZVP — tier ‘standard’ in chemkit (density-fitted).
Geometry provenance: fixed geometry supplied by user at /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/acrolein/acrolein.xyz (no re-optimization; single-point population analyses only).
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvation model).
Charge & multiplicity: 0, 1 (neutral singlet); cation/anionic states computed as charge ±1, multiplicity 2 to obtain N±1 populations.
Software/backend: PySCF 2.x via chemkit fukui-reactivity skill (Mulliken charges).
Convergence & caveats: SCF cycles for neutral, cationic, and anionic states all reported as converged. Density fitting (RIJK) approximation active by default. The integrity block returned status "ok" and trustworthy = true.  Warnings from the engine are reproduced verbatim below.

Headline result (most electrophilic site)
• Atom 4 (index 3, C of the carbonyl carbon) has the largest f⁺ = 0.64232102 (dimensionless).
Most nucleophilic site: Atom 1 (index 0, O) with f⁻ = 0.39388616.
